source_intake_record.raw_question_text: For the combination of one prism and 2 lenses shown (Fig. 1.42), find the location and size of the final image when the object, length 1 cm, is located as shown in the figure.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: $10 \mathrm{cm}$ to the left of the second lens; $0.5 \mathrm{cm}$
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image placeholder string rather than a concrete file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image placeholder string rather than a concrete file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式引用“shown (Fig. 1.42)”并要求使用图中物体位置与光学元件组合信息。
requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，说明关键信息主要来自图像而非文字。
问题涉及一个棱镜和两个透镜的相对布置、距离与成像方向，这些通常无法仅凭题干文字完整恢复。
multi_step: 要求同时求最终像的位置和大小，至少包含连续成像/放大率计算两个相关目标。
光学系统包含 1 个棱镜和 2 个透镜，通常需要按元件顺序进行多步传播与中间像分析。
题干中的“object ... is located as shown in the figure”表明需要结合图中几何位置再做后续计算，不是单一步代公式。
verifiability: 答案是明确的数值结果：最终像位置“10 cm to the left of the second lens”和尺寸“0.5 cm”，可核对性较强。
该题属于标准几何光学成像，若图中参数可读，后续可用透镜公式和放大率规则验证过程与结果。
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true，且题目与答案都存在，基础核验条件较好。

candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image placeholder string rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: For the right-angle prism, $n=1.5$, the critical angle $\alpha=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)=$ $42^{\circ}$, which is smaller than the angle of incidence, $45^{\circ}$, at the hypotenuse of the prism. Therefore total internal reflection occurs, which forms a virtual image. The prism, equivalent to a glass plate of thickness 6 cm, would cause an image shift of $$\Delta L=6\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)=2 \mathrm{~cm}$$ Thus the effective object distance for the first lens is $$\nu_{1}=10+6+(6-2)=20 \mathrm{~cm}$$ As $u_{1}=f_{1}$, we have $v_{1}=\infty$. Then for the second lens, $u_{2}=\infty$. With $f_{2}=-10 \mathrm{~cm}$, we get $$v_{2}=f_{2}=-10 \mathrm{~cm}$$ Hence the final image is inverted and virtual, 10 cm to the left of the second lens. The size of the image is $$l_{2}=\left|\frac{f_{2}}{f_{1}}\right| \cdot 1=0.5 \mathrm{~cm}$$
